summary.what_happened: Notification sends began failing at 09:42 with template rendering errors; the error rate recovered after 10:20.
summary.customer_impact: Patient notifications were delayed (queued for retry); no booking functionality was affected.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-15T09:55:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; end = start of sustained recovery across all deviated series

severity.explanation: send error rate peaked at 9.1% but notifications are retried from a dead-letter queue and are not on the booking critical path; latency stayed at baseline and the incident recovered within the window

timeline.description: deploy 2026.06.13-1105 on notifications-service: Add rich template rendering engine (dormant behind rich_templates flag)
feature_flag flags/rich_templates@on on notifications-service: Enable rich_templates: new placeholder-based template rendering for patient notifications
ERROR: TemplateRenderError: unknown placeholder 'patient_name' in template welcome_v2
ERROR: TemplateRenderError: unknown placeholder 'patient_name' in template reminder_v2
WARN: Send failure ratio above 4% over last 5m; failing sends are all rich-template renders
ERROR: TemplateRenderError: unknown placeholder 'patient_name' in template welcome_v2
Alert fired: Error rate spike on notifications-service send endpoint
ERROR: TemplateRenderError: unknown placeholder 'clinic_address' in template confirmation_v2
ERROR: TemplateRenderError: unknown placeholder 'patient_name' in template welcome_v2
WARN: Dead-letter queue growing: 1,204 undeliverable render failures queued for retry
feature_flag flags/rich_templates@off on notifications-service: Disable rich_templates (on-call rollback of cfg_774)

evidence.interpretation: feature_flag rich_templates@on at 09:40 precedes first errors by ~2 minutes; flag@off at 10:20 precedes recovery by ~5 minutes - alignment in both directions
the 06-13 deploy shipped the rendering engine dormant behind the flag, so the code change predates the incident but its activation does not
TemplateRenderError for placeholder 'patient_name' appears at 09:42:38, two minutes after the rich_templates flag was enabled at 09:40:12
flag disabled by on-call at 10:20:05; the next send batch at 10:24:41 succeeded on the legacy template path
error_rate_pct first crossed the 2x-baseline rule at 09:45 (0.2% -> 4.8%), peaked at 9.1%, and returned to baseline from 10:25
p95 latency stayed at baseline throughout - failures are fast, pointing away from a capacity or dependency problem
email-gateway error rate stayed at baseline, ruling the gateway out
runbook failure mode 'template rendering errors after template or flag changes' matches: TemplateRenderError with flat latency
failing sends error inside render_template in ~10ms; the email-gateway deliver span is absent from failing traces - failures never reach the gateway

hypotheses.title: rich_templates flag enabled templates with unprovided placeholders
hypotheses.statement: Enabling rich_templates at 09:40 activated templates referencing placeholders the send context does not provide, failing renders fast; disabling the flag at 10:20 restored sends, consistent with the flag being the trigger.
assumptions: telemetry in the package is complete for the window
recommended_checks: diff welcome_v2/reminder_v2 placeholders against the send context fields
confirm dead-letter retry drain completed without duplicate sends

missing_data.description: runbook_investigator: dead-letter queue depth limit before notification delay becomes user-visible
missing_data.impact: flagged by an investigator as limiting its conclusions

recommended_next_steps.description: diff welcome_v2/reminder_v2 placeholders against the send context fields
confirm dead-letter retry drain completed without duplicate sends
Investigate open question: runbook_investigator: dead-letter queue depth limit before notification delay becomes user-visible

safe_mitigation_options.action: Keep rich_templates disabled until template placeholders are reconciled with the send context
safe_mitigation_options.rationale: the flag flip is the aligned trigger; legacy path is healthy
risks: rich template features unavailable until fixed

safe_mitigation_options.action: Drain the dead-letter queue in batches during business hours
safe_mitigation_options.rationale: runbook documents batch drain to avoid duplicate-send spikes
risks: duplicate sends if drained too fast

remediation_plans.title: Consider a guarded re-enable process for rich_templates
preconditions: operator flag revert at 10:20 is holding (error rate at baseline)

steps.action: diff template placeholders against send-context fields
steps.verification: every placeholder resolves against the context schema

steps.action: drain the dead-letter queue in business-hours batches
steps.verification: queue depth decreases batch by batch with no duplicate-send reports

abort_conditions: duplicate notifications reported during the drain

signals.recovered_when: within 10% of baseline 0.2 % for >= 3 consecutive points

log_patterns_should_stop: TemplateRenderError: unknown placeholder 'patient_name' in template welcome_vN
recovery_verification.re_alert_condition: error_rate_pct on notifications-service crosses the alert threshold 2 again

checks.detail: SEV-3 is within the numeric ceiling (SEV-2)

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-3, recovered: notification sends failed at up to 9% between 09:42 and 10:20 due to template rendering errors after the rich_templates flag was enabled; on-call disabled the flag and sends recovered. 1,204 notifications are queued for retry. No remediation beyond the already-performed operator flag revert has been executed; queue-drain options await human approval.

jira_ticket.summary: Reconcile notification templates with send context after flag incident
jira_ticket.description: Notification send error rate peaked at 9.1% between 09:42Z and 10:20Z (recovered in-window after the operator reverted rich_templates). Leading hypothesis (high confidence): templates referenced placeholders absent from the send context. 1,204 notifications queued for retry. Affected: notifications-service.

slack_update.text: SEV-3, recovered: notification sends failed at up to 9% for ~40 minutes after rich_templates was enabled; on-call reverted the flag and sends recovered. 1,204 notifications queued for retry. No remediation has been executed by the tool; queue-drain options await human approval.

status_page.text: Some notification emails were delayed earlier today. Delivery has returned to normal and delayed messages are being retried. We are monitoring to confirm full recovery.

postmortem_draft.title: Postmortem draft: notification send failures 2026-06-15
postmortem_draft.summary: Enabling the rich_templates flag activated templates that referenced placeholders the send context did not provide; renders failed fast at up to 9.1% of sends until the flag was reverted 40 minutes later.
postmortem_draft.impact: ~40 minutes of degraded notification delivery; 1,204 notifications delayed into retry; no booking impact.
contributing_factors: likely: templates and send-context fields were not validated together before the flag was enabled
open_questions: why template validation did not run against production contexts
action_items: diff template placeholders against send-context fields before re-enabling
add a render smoke test to the flag-enable checklist

reasoning_trace.summary: derived from 1 deviated series and 1 recurring error pattern(s); mode=confirm_sustained_recovery per the incident window rule
applied documented severity rules; recovery and workaround observed
combined aligned findings across sources | timing: 'rich_templates flag enabled templates with unprovided placeholders': flag on 09:40 -> errors 09:42; flag off 10:20 -> recovery 10:25
statements stay within what the cited evidence shows (4 checks, 0 non-pass)
aggregated 3 next steps deterministically (2 from hypothesis checks, 1 from missing data)
drafted from the reviewed flag-rollback narrative (2 mitigation option(s) proposed)
structured the reviewed options into guided plans (1 plan(s), 1 state-changing step(s), all requiring approval)
deterministic lint: 10 checks (total review: 0 blocked, 0 warning)
